name: Cloudflare
api_notes: The Cloudflare API is a comprehensive REST-based interface that allows control over nearly all Cloudflare products and account settings. The API documentation has transitioned to the OpenAPI specification.
access_notes: Developers can sign up for a Cloudflare account for free and generate API tokens directly from their dashboard to access the API.
one_liner: Cloudflare provides a comprehensive global cloud platform that includes edge computing, security, storage, and networking services, all of which are manageable via their public API.
mcp_notes: No official or well-known community Model Context Protocol (MCP) server was found for the Cloudflare API.
auth_notes: Cloudflare primarily uses API tokens (which function as Bearer tokens) and API keys for authentication. Documentation for authentication is provided for every endpoint.

evidence.claim: Cloudflare provides an extensive API Reference covering account management, security settings, and product configurations.
Cloudflare has standardized its API documentation using the OpenAPI specification to improve developer experience.
Cloudflare provides a unified developer platform to build, deploy, and manage applications globally.
Search citation: APIs · Cloudflare Workers docs
